text.title: Workflow Spec Phase Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example showing how workflow-spec-phase produces a valid spec.md (Draft) artefact.

example.title: Happy-path application: JWT refresh feature
example.code:
--- artefact_id: spec-jwt-refresh owner: Ruslan Faion <ruslan@faion.net> version: 1.0.0 last_reviewed: 2026-05-23 feature: jwt-refresh status: Draft ---
# Spec: JWT Refresh Endpoint
## Functional Requirements
- FR-3: System SHALL rotate the refresh token on every successful POST /api/v1/auth/refresh. - FR-4: System SHALL reject any refresh token that has already been used (replay protection).
## Non-Functional Requirements
- NFR-2: Refresh endpoint p95 latency SHALL be under 200ms at 100 RPS.
## In Scope
- POST /api/v1/auth/refresh endpoint - Token rotation logic - Tests for FR-3, FR-4
## Out of Scope
- OAuth federation - Session-based fallback for browsers without JS
## Success Criteria
- ≥99% successful refresh rate in production over 7 days post-ship - p95 latency < 200ms at expected load
## Glossary
- **refresh token** — Long-lived token used to obtain a new short-lived access token via /auth/refresh. - **rotation** — Issuing a new refresh token alongside each new access token, invalidating the old one.
## Open Questions
(none recorded — clarify phase yielded no unresolved items)
> Status: Draft — awaiting human owner review for Accepted promotion.
example.note: Demonstrates the rules from 01-core-rules.xml applied to an intake note; output validates against the schema in 02-output-contract.xml.
